behaviors.description: Generated function signature matches expected interface from the natural-language spec.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is about model-produced code, not just local parsing. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on what the real model outputs for parameters/types/return type. MULTI-STEP: not required; this behavior can be checked on a single generation call. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because behavior text only requires signature correctness per generation, not a chained multi-decision loop.
behaviors.test_description: Run real LLM generation for a set of interface-constrained prompts, compile/inspect the produced function signature, and assert exact match on parameter names, types, and return type.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real model output quality; lower-level stubs could hide signature mistakes.

behaviors.description: Orchestration runs attempts in order and exits early on first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (orchestrator + executor + bookkeeping), not pure single-function logic. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required if test executor is in-process fake. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model output quality when using deterministic attempt outcomes. Rejection-gate: Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text is control-flow ordering and early-return logic, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator with deterministic stub generator and stub test runner that returns fail/pass by attempt index; assert call order and immediate stop after first pass.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test exercises cross-module orchestration boundary; unit-only check would miss wiring regressions.

behaviors.description: Live iterative loop can recover and pass within allowed attempts for a case where one-shot usually fails.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: success depends on real model behavior. MULTI-STEP: explicitly requires sequential generate->test->refine->retest decisions across attempts. MOCK-HIDE: deterministic stubs can force success/failure and hide whether real iterative recovery works. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected as minimum because behavior text requires the iterative loop across multiple attempts, not a single model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real LLM and real loop for a benchmark task known to often fail on first try; verify that within <=3 attempts the pipeline reaches a passing implementation and records attempt progression.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs multiple real agentic steps; lower levels cannot validate iterative recovery behavior.

behaviors.description: After test failures, refinement prompt builder includes failed test names and failure messages in the next prompt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: key risk is state handoff from test results module into prompt-builder module. LLM-DEP: prompt content check is deterministic and does not require judging model output quality. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text is about prompt construction content, not whether model uses it well.
behaviors.test_description: Run failed test results through real prompt-builder integration path and assert the next prompt payload contains exact failed test identifiers and failure messages.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Cross-module handoff is essential; unit-only string-template test could miss integration mapping bugs.

behaviors.description: Next live attempt changes implementation in response to prior failure context instead of resubmitting identical code.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: requires real model behavior under refinement context. MULTI-STEP: needs at least two agentic generations linked by prior failure feedback. MOCK-HIDE: stubs can trivially return different code and mask whether feedback actually drives change. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected as minimum because behavior text requires sequential dependence on prior-step failure context.
behaviors.test_description: Execute two-attempt live loop with real LLM where first attempt fails; assert second generated implementation is materially different and aligned to prior failure signal (not a duplicate submission).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires chained model decisions; single-step agentic test is insufficient.

behaviors.description: Service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns structured error containing all attempts and test results.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure occurs in orchestrator + attempt ledger + error serializer interaction. LLM-DEP: no model quality dependence; deterministic fail stubs are sufficient. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network required for this boundary. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because behavior text is deterministic stop/aggregation logic after fixed retries.
behaviors.test_description: Use deterministic failing generator/test-runner stubs for three attempts, then assert termination at attempt 3 and exact structured error schema with per-attempt test outcomes.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is needed for retry bookkeeping and response assembly across modules.

behaviors.description: Full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end-to-end with deterministic stubs.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly says non-agentic and deterministic stubs, so LLM-DEP is disqualified by text. MOCK-MISS: target is end-to-end module wiring and state propagation. REAL-INFRA: System only if insisting on real subprocess/network; behavior text does not require that. Rejection-gate: Agentic/Workflow rejected by explicit 'non-agentic' + 'deterministic stubbed model outputs'.
behaviors.test_description: Run full pipeline in-process with deterministic stub generator outputs and controlled test executor responses; assert generation invocation, execution results, and attempt bookkeeping are consistent end-to-end.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Chosen level matches explicit non-agentic wiring goal; unit tests alone would miss cross-module breakage.

behaviors.description: Full live loop solves at least one known hard function that fails on first attempt.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: relies on real model producing improved code. MULTI-STEP: explicit chained loop generate->test->refine->retest across attempts. MOCK-HIDE: stubs cannot validate real iterative problem-solving capability on hard task. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected as minimum because behavior text requires end-to-end multi-attempt live loop success, not single-step generation quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real end-to-end loop on a curated hard prompt with known first-attempt failure tendency; require eventual pass within retry budget and verify first attempt failed before later success.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs real multi-step agent pipeline behavior; lower levels cannot catch this class of failure.
